what_it_does: Implements an adaptive, data-driven intake interview engine exposed as window.MS_INTAKE. It models a project brief as knowledge areas with required/optional fields, computes a confidence-weighted understanding score, drives a branching list of interview moves, supplies project-aware option sets and acknowledgements, and decides when the system has enough context to begin synthesis or generation.
observations: The engine is wrapped in an IIFE assigned to window.MS_INTAKE and returns a public API.
AREAS define knowledge domains (aim, orient, audience, substance, feel, anchors) with required/optional fields and weights.
understanding() returns an overall score and a per-area confidence map; canSynthesize() gates generation with hard-coded thresholds.
MOVES is an ordered, conditional question list: each move has a when predicate, target field, kind, Vi prompt, helper text, and an options generator.
Option sets include static arrays (GOALS, BUILD_TYPES, SITUATION, INTENTS, STAKEHOLDERS, JOURNEY, CONTENT, TONES, MUST_HAVE) and dynamic generators (avoidOptions, jobExamples, recommendBuildType).
Input kinds include cards, text, multi, scale, upload, dual, and refs, though their rendering and validation are not shown here.
ack() produces adaptive, value-specific acknowledgement copy with random variants for several fields.
avoidOptions() reads from window.MS_DATA?.rejectionPatterns, coupling the engine to an external data global.
Several brief fields declared in emptyBrief (intent, existingFlow, direction, renderer, sequenceDraft, title) are never collected by the moves in this file.
The audience area and the stakeholder/journey/task moves depend on brief.intent, but no move asks for intent.
standouts: Confidence-weighted area model used both for progress and for a synthesis gate.
Project-aware option generation that changes examples and recommendations based on prior answers.
Conditional move ordering that branches the interview without explicit state-machine wiring.
Rich natural-language acknowledgements that reference the user's actual answer.
conventions: Global namespace attachment via an IIFE on window.
Lookup tables as arrays of {id, label, desc?, icon?} objects.
Label helpers resolve ids to lower-case, article-stripped strings for sentence insertion.
Functional, class-free style with mostly pure helpers.
Section comments describing architecture above code blocks.
concerns: No move collects intent, existingFlow, or the synthesis fields (direction, renderer, sequenceDraft, title), so the interview as defined cannot fully populate the brief it models.
Hard-coded numeric thresholds in canSynthesize may drift out of sync with AREAS or MOVES changes.
ack() uses Math.random(), making the same answer produce different acknowledgement copy.
avoidOptions() depends on window.MS_DATA being present; while it falls back to an empty array, the anti-pattern memory concept is not defined here.
Stakeholder/journey/task moves are gated on intent, but intent defaults to null and is never set within this file, so those questions may be silently skipped.
notes: This file is the interview conductor: it decides what to ask next and when the gathered context is strong enough to start generating.
questions: Where is brief.intent set, given no move asks for it?
Are direction, renderer, sequenceDraft, and title filled by a later synthesis module?
Which component renders the move kinds (cards, dual, upload, refs) and enforces max selection rules?
How are uploaded sources processed and stored?
Are the canSynthesize thresholds configurable or derived from area metadata?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—an adaptive, project-aware interview that gates generation until understanding is sufficient—is largely realized by the area model, conditional moves, and synthesis gate. The gap is that some brief fields (intent, existingFlow, synthesis fields) are declared but not collected by the moves here, so the interview path in this file alone does not fully realize the brief schema.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make an automated intake feel like a perceptive collaborator: it narrows the project from a high-level outcome, tailors the next question and options to what it already knows, refuses to jump to output until it understands enough, and responds to each answer with context-aware language.
novelty: A weighted area-confidence model combined with conditional move predicates and project-aware option generators to drive a synthesis-gated conversational intake.
contribution: It contributes a reusable intake-engine pattern: a structured brief schema, confidence-weighted knowledge areas, conditional interview moves, adaptive acknowledgements, and a generation-readiness gate that a fused system could keep to drive goal-first project discovery.
summary_for_embedding: Adaptive intake engine that scores project-brief understanding across weighted knowledge areas, drives a conditional interview through project-aware moves and options, and gates synthesis until confidence thresholds are met.
intention_for_embedding: To let a design assistant conduct a goal-first, branching interview that gathers enough context before proposing or generating outputs.

symbols.does: Returns the initial empty project brief object.
symbols.description: Constructs a brief with all intake fields set to null or empty values, plus later synthesis placeholders such as direction, renderer, sequenceDraft, and title.

symbols.does: Checks whether a brief field has a non-empty value.
symbols.description: Handles arrays, strings, and null/undefined values so area confidence can treat empty arrays or whitespace-only strings as unfilled.

symbols.does: Computes the confidence of a single knowledge area.
symbols.description: Returns null when the area's when predicate is false, otherwise scores required fields (or optional fields for optional-only areas) as the fraction filled.

symbols.does: Computes overall and per-area understanding scores.
symbols.description: Aggregates areaConfidence results weighted by area.weight, ignoring N/A areas, and returns an overall 0..1 score plus a per-area map.
Returns true only when aim and orient are fully complete, substance is mostly complete, and at least one of audience or feel has crossed a threshold.

symbols.does: Builds the avoid-options list from live rejection patterns.
symbols.description: Maps window.MS_DATA.rejectionPatterns into id/label options, falling back to an empty array when the global data is absent.

symbols.does: Returns example job-to-be-done phrases tailored to the brief.
symbols.description: Selects chip text based on buildType and goal so the user sees concrete verbs relevant to the project form they chose.

symbols.does: Maps a goal to a suggested build type id.
symbols.description: Provides a simple heuristic used to highlight a default choice in the buildType move, such as agent for automate or brochure for present.

symbols.does: Resolves a stakeholder id to a lower-case label.
symbols.description: Looks up the id in STAKEHOLDERS and returns a sentence-friendly form, defaulting to 'user'.

symbols.does: Resolves a goal id to a lower-case label.
symbols.description: Looks up the id in GOALS; if the id is actually a freeform string, returns it lower-cased so prompts can quote the user's own words.

symbols.does: Resolves an intent id to a quoted lower-case label.
symbols.description: Looks up the id in INTENTS and wraps the label in curly quotes for use in prompts.

symbols.does: Resolves a build type id to a lower-case label.
symbols.description: Looks up the id in BUILD_TYPES and strips a leading article so the phrase fits naturally inside recommendations and prompts.

symbols.does: Returns the next unhandled applicable move.
symbols.description: Iterates MOVES in order and returns the first move whose id is not in the handled set and whose when predicate passes, or null when none remain.

symbols.does: Counts how many moves are currently applicable.
symbols.description: Filters MOVES by their when predicates to give a denominator for progress indication.

symbols.does: Produces an adaptive acknowledgement for a captured answer.
symbols.description: Switches on field name to return value-specific, often randomised copy that references the user's actual answer and sets up the next question.

symbols.does: Selects a random element from an array.
symbols.description: Small local helper used by ack() to vary acknowledgement copy for fields like goalNote and jobToBeDone.

symbols.does: Resolves a tone id to its display label.
symbols.description: Looks up the id in TONES and returns the raw id as fallback.

symbols.does: Truncates a string to a maximum length with an ellipsis.
symbols.description: Guards against null/undefined and appends '…' only when the string exceeds the limit, used when quoting user text in acknowledgements.
